figures.caption: Figure 2: Standard Galerkin solution for the 1D advection-diffusion problem (39) (Test 2) with ε = 10−5, p = 5, and ne = 512.

Figure 3: Comparison of stabilized solutions for the 1D advection-diffusion problem (39) (Test 2) using p = 5 and ne = 512. (a) Numerical solutions over the full domain using SUPG and Multilevel stabilization. (b) Detailed zoom near the boundary layer (x = 1).
Figure 4: Relative L2-error convergence plot for the 1D advection-diffusion problem (39) (Test 2) with polynomial degree p = 5.
Figure 7: Standard Galerkin surface plot for the parabolic boundary layers problem (1) (Test 3) with p = 5 and ne = 64× 64.
Figure 8: Multilevel stabilized surface plot for the parabolic boundary layers problem (1) (Test 3) with p = 5 and ne = 64× 64.
Figure 9: Comparison of stabilized solutions for the parabolic boundary layers problem (1) (Test 3) with ne = 64× 64. (a) Polynomial degree p = 5. (b) Polynomial degree p = 3.
Figure 10: Surface plots of the numerical solutions for the two internal layers problem (1) (Test 4) with p = 3 and ne = 64× 64. (a) Multilevel stabilized solution. (b) Standard Galerkin solution.

Figure 11: Cross-sectional profiles at x = 0.5 for the two internal layers problem (1) (Test 4) with p = 3, ne = 64× 64, and L = 5. (a) Comparison between Multilevel and SUPG methods. (b) Comparison between Multilevel method and the reference solution.
Figure 12: SUPG surface plot for the rotational problem (1) (Test 5) with p = 3 and ne = 64× 64.
Figure 13: Multilevel stabilized surface plot for the rotational problem (1) (Test 5) with p = 3 and ne = 64× 64.
Figure 14: Surface plots for the 2D pure advection problem (40) (Test 6) using ne = (8, 256). (a) Exact solution. (b) Standard Galerkin solution with polynomial degrees p = (2, 3).

Figure 15: Cross-sectional profiles for the 2D pure advection problem (40) (Test 6) with polynomial degrees p = (2, 5) and mesh size ne = (8, 256). (a) Multilevel stabilized solution. (b) Standard Galerkin solution.
Figure 16: Relative error convergence plots for the 2D pure advection problem (40) (Test 6) with different polynomial degrees. (a) Relative L2-norm error. (b) Relative H1-norm error.
Figure 17: Condition number comparison for the pure advection problem across different polynomial degrees.
